description:
You are a ground systems engineer validating a shared baseband router for a dense satellite constellation. Perform a formal 'Isolation Verification' between the INST and INST2 simulated satellites using OpenC3 COSMOS (Password: Cosmos2024!) to ensure commands to one do not affect the other. Conduct a bidirectional test by sending COLLECT commands (TYPE NORMAL, DURATION 1) to each satellite and monitoring the HEALTH_STATUS COLLECTS telemetry counters for both. Document the initial and final telemetry states, the commands sent, and the isolation results for both test phases in a JSON report saved to /home/ga/Desktop/isolation_audit.json. The report must follow this schema:

{
  "operator": "ga",
  "test_timestamp": "<ISO 8601 datetime string>",
  "phase1_command_inst": {
     "inst_initial_collects": <int>,
     "inst2_initial_collects": <int>,
     "command_sent": "<string>",
     "inst_final_collects": <int>,
     "inst2_final_collects": <int>,
     "inst_incremented": <bool>,
     "inst2_isolated": <bool>
  },
  "phase2_command_inst2": {
     "inst_initial_collects": <int>,
     "inst2_initial_collects": <int>,
     "command_sent": "<string>",
     "inst_final_collects": <int>,
     "inst2_final_collects": <int>,
     "inst2_incremented": <bool>,
     "inst_isolated": <bool>
  },
  "system_isolated": <bool>
}
